description: Template Service Phase 1 — Project scaffold, PostgreSQL schema migrations, typed repository layer, JWT+RBAC auth plugin. Backend only (apps/platform/template). No Redis, no BullMQ, no EventBridge.

userStories.title: Project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Template Service directory skeleton so subsequent stories have a working base to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/platform/template/ with package.json: name @platform/template, version 0.1.0, type module, engines.node >=24; dependencies: fastify@^5.0.0, @fastify/sensible@^6.0.0, knex@^3.0.0, pg@^8.0.0, @sinclair/typebox@^0.34.0, jose@^5.0.0, lru-cache@^11.0.0; devDependencies: tsx@^4.0.0, typescript@^5.0.0, @types/node@^22.0.0, @types/pg@^8.0.0, vitest@^2.0.0
scripts in package.json: build (tsc), dev (tsx watch src/index.ts), start (node dist/index.js), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (vitest run), test:watch (vitest)
tsconfig.json: compilerOptions target ESNext, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, strict true, outDir ./dist, rootDir ./src, esModuleInterop true, skipLibCheck true; include ["src/**/*"]

src/config.ts: uses TypeBox Type.Object + Value.Check to validate env: DATABASE_URL (required string), JWT_SECRET (required string), PORT (optional integer); if Value.Check fails, extract first error path and throw Error('Missing required env: <field>'); exports typed config object with PORT defaulting to 3005

src/app.ts: exports async buildApp(db: Knex, jwtSecret: string): Promise<FastifyInstance>; registers @fastify/sensible; decorates app with db and jwtSecret; registers GET /health route returning 200 { status: 'ok' }

src/index.ts: imports buildApp and config; creates knex connection (client: 'pg', connection: config.DATABASE_URL, searchPath: ['platform_templates']); calls buildApp(db, config.JWT_SECRET); when NODE_ENV !== 'test' calls app.listen({ port: config.PORT, host: '0.0.0.0' }) and logs errors to app.log; exports app for test imports
src/types.d.ts: augments FastifyInstance interface (via declare module 'fastify') to add db: Knex and jwtSecret: string decorators; imports Knex type from 'knex'
Dockerfile: FROM node:24-alpine, WORKDIR /app, COPY package.json ., RUN npm ci --omit=dev, COPY dist/ ./dist/, CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
.env.example lists: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, PORT=3005
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No Redis, no BullMQ, no EventBridge. Follow email service patterns: buildApp factory, TypeBox env validation, @fastify/sensible v6. PORT default 3005.

userStories.title: Database migrations — platform_templates schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the platform_templates schema with correct tables and indexes so the service can persist and query templates and their version history.
acceptanceCriteria: migrations/knexfile.ts: exports default Knex.Config with client 'pg', connection process.env.DATABASE_URL, searchPath ['platform_templates', 'public'], migrations.directory '.', migrations.schemaName 'platform_templates', migrations.tableName 'knex_migrations', migrations.loadExtensions ['.ts']

migrations/001_create_templates.ts: up() calls knex.raw('CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS platform_templates'); creates table platform_templates.templates with columns: id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), name text NOT NULL UNIQUE, channel text NOT NULL CHECK (channel IN ('sms','email')), status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK (status IN ('draft','active','disabled')), active_version integer, current_version integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, created_by uuid, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(); creates index templates_channel_idx on templates(channel); creates index templates_status_idx on templates(status); creates index templates_updated_at_idx on templates(updated_at DESC); creates index templates_created_at_idx on templates(created_at DESC)
migrations/001_create_templates.ts: down() drops table platform_templates.templates if exists

migrations/002_create_template_versions.ts: up() creates table platform_templates.template_versions with columns: id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), template_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES platform_templates.templates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, version integer NOT NULL, body_text text, subject text, body_html text, body_unlayer jsonb, created_by uuid, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(); adds UNIQUE constraint on (template_id, version); creates index template_versions_template_id_idx on template_versions(template_id)
migrations/002_create_template_versions.ts: down() drops table platform_templates.template_versions if exists
Migrations run successfully against a Postgres instance (npx knex --knexfile migrations/knexfile.ts migrate:latest)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use knex.raw() for CREATE SCHEMA and raw index DDL. Use knex.schema.withSchema('platform_templates') for createTable. Migrations must run in order: 001 before 002 due to FK reference.

userStories.title: Templates repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed repository layer for platform_templates DB access so routes and services never write raw SQL outside the repository.
acceptanceCriteria: src/repositories/templates.ts exports TemplateRow interface matching templates table: id, name, channel, status, active_version (number | null), current_version, created_by (string | null), created_at (string), updated_at (string)

src/repositories/templates.ts exports TemplateVersionRow interface matching template_versions table: id, template_id, version, body_text (string | null), subject (string | null), body_html (string | null), body_unlayer (unknown | null), created_by (string | null), created_at (string)
Exports TemplatesRepo class taking a Knex instance in constructor; all queries use withSchema('platform_templates')
findById(id: string): Promise<TemplateRow | null> — select * from templates where id = id; return null if not found
findByName(name: string): Promise<TemplateRow | null> — select * from templates where name = name; return null if not found

list(opts: { channel?: string, status?: string, sort?: 'created_at' | 'updated_at', order?: 'asc' | 'desc', limit?: number, offset?: number }): Promise<{ rows: TemplateRow[], total: number }> — builds base query with optional channel/status filters; runs two queries: count query for total (no limit/offset), data query with orderBy(sort ?? 'updated_at', order ?? 'desc') and limit/offset applied

create(data: { name: string, channel: string, created_by?: string | null }): Promise<TemplateRow> — wraps in knex.transaction; inserts templates row returning all columns; inserts template_versions row (template_id: template.id, version: 1, all content fields null, created_by: data.created_by ?? null); returns the template row

findVersionContent(templateId: string, version: number): Promise<TemplateVersionRow | null> — select * from template_versions where template_id = templateId and version = version; return null if not found

updateVersionInPlace(templateId: string, version: number, data: Partial<Pick<TemplateVersionRow, 'body_text' | 'subject' | 'body_html' | 'body_unlayer'>>): Promise<void> — updates template_versions row at (template_id, version) with provided fields

insertNewVersion(data: { template_id: string, version: number, body_text?: string | null, subject?: string | null, body_html?: string | null, body_unlayer?: unknown | null, created_by?: string | null }): Promise<void> — inserts new template_versions row

updateTemplateGroup(id: string, data: Partial<Pick<TemplateRow, 'name' | 'status' | 'active_version' | 'current_version' | 'updated_at'>>): Promise<TemplateRow> — updates templates row with provided fields; sets updated_at to now() if not explicitly in data; returns updated row via returning('*')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No business logic in repository — only DB access. All queries scoped to platform_templates schema. updated_at is always refreshed in updateTemplateGroup even if not passed by caller.

userStories.title: Auth plugin — JWT verification and role guards
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable auth utilities so all template routes can enforce JWT authentication and RBAC roles without duplicating verification logic.
acceptanceCriteria: src/plugins/auth.ts: exports JwtClaims interface { sub: string, roles?: string[] }

exports verifyJwt(authHeader: string | undefined, secret: string): Promise<JwtClaims> — extracts Bearer token from Authorization header; calls jose jwtVerify with HS256; returns payload cast to JwtClaims; throws fastify.httpErrors.unauthorized('Unauthorized') if header missing; throws fastify.httpErrors.unauthorized('Invalid token') on jose verification failure
exports isServiceApiKey(authHeader: string | undefined): boolean — returns true if Authorization header is present and the token portion (after 'Bearer ') starts with 'ak_'

exports requireAuth(secret: string): FastifyRequestHandler — preHandler that accepts either a valid user JWT OR a service API key; calls isServiceApiKey first, if true returns (allowed); otherwise calls verifyJwt; throws 401 if neither passes

exports requireRole(role: string, secret: string): FastifyRequestHandler — preHandler that calls verifyJwt (never accepts API keys); checks roles array includes role; throws fastify.httpErrors.forbidden('Forbidden') if role not present; throws 401 if token invalid

src/app.ts updated: buildApp signature unchanged (jwtSecret already a param); after decorating app with jwtSecret, imports and registers auth helpers as app-level utilities so route handlers can call app.requireAuth() and app.requireRole(role) without passing the secret manually — either export factory functions bound to jwtSecret, or document usage pattern in code comment

test/unit/auth.test.ts: signs test JWTs using jose SignJWT with HS256; tests: (1) valid JWT with roles: ['marketing_staff'] passes requireRole('marketing_staff'); (2) valid JWT with roles: ['marketing_staff'] fails requireRole('marketing_manager') with 403; (3) missing Authorization header throws 401; (4) JWT with wrong signature throws 401; (5) service API key 'Bearer ak_test123' passes requireAuth; (6) random non-JWT non-ak_ bearer token fails requireAuth with 401
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: @ortho/auth-middleware does not exist as a package — implement auth inline using jose. JWT claims include sub (user ID) and roles (string[]). Service API keys start with 'Bearer ak_'. Both requireAuth and requireRole throw using @fastify/sensible httpErrors for consistent error shapes.
